goal: Complete RLS-1 owner mapping and RLS-2 dependency mapping for the confirmed 71 RLS-disabled public tables before any RLS policy or grant changes.

allowed_now: Read table comments, column lists, grants, policies, and row counts.
Read workflow/config references needed to identify active clients.
Run read-only sample queries only where approved and only enough to confirm owner attribution.
forbidden_without_explicit_followup_appr: Update, insert, delete, or backfill rows.
Print secret values or raw sensitive row contents.
Treat shared agent_* or conv_* tables as APEX-only.

required_outputs: RLS owner map grouped by table, project code/domain, ownership evidence, and confidence.
Dependency map showing active n8n/Make/workflow/API/client usage for each exposed table group.
Risk-ranked rollout batches with no policy SQL applied.
Tables requiring operator decision because ownership or client usage is ambiguous.
Smoke-test inventory needed before any future RLS or grant change.
minimum_queries: List public tables, relrowsecurity, relforcerowsecurity, estimated row count, and table comment.
List grants for anon, authenticated, and service_role by table and privilege.
List columns likely to identify ownership: project_id, user_id, owner_id, org_id, entity_id, series_id, created_by.
For shared coordination tables, count rows by project_id without printing row bodies.
success_criteria: Every one of the 71 RLS-disabled tables has an owner classification or explicit unknown marker.
Every table group has known or unknown active clients listed.
No raw secret values or sensitive row contents are captured.
Next RLS-3 policy drafting packet can be produced from the owner/dependency map.
rollback_plan: No production changes are authorized by this packet. If an accidental write is attempted, stop immediately, record the command and result, and escalate with the affected object name and transaction/statement evidence.

stop_for: Need to inspect sensitive row contents
Ambiguous table ownership that cannot be resolved from metadata or safe aggregate counts
blocker_policy.safe_fallback_path: Produce a partial owner/dependency map with unknowns and do not advance to policy drafting.
